- add-jit-kernel: Step-by-step tutorial for adding a new lightweight JIT CUDA kernel to sglang's jit_kernel module
- add-sgl-kernel: Step-by-step tutorial for adding a heavyweight AOT CUDA/C++ kernel to sgl-kernel (including tests & benchmarks)
- ci-workflow-guide: Guide to SGLang CI workflow orchestration — stage ordering, fast-fail, gating, partitioning, execution modes, and debugging CI failures. Use when modifying CI workflows, adding stages, debugging CI pipeline issues, or understanding how tests are dispatched and gated across stages.
- cookbook-migrate-model: Migrate a legacy-template SGLang cookbook page (monolithic per-model generator under docs_new/src/snippets/autoregressive/) onto the config-driven template (shared _deployment.jsx / _playground.jsx engines + per-model config). Use when asked to migrate, convert, or port an existing cookbook page — NOT for brand-new models (use cookbook-add-model for those). Run with /cookbook-migrate-model <Model page name, e.g. GLM-5.1>.
- cookbook-review-pr: Review a pull request against the SGLang Cookbook (docs_new/, Mintlify) contribution checklist — the config-driven format (per-model config + benchmarks JSX consumed by the shared _deployment.jsx / _playground.jsx engines). Run with /cookbook-review-pr <PR number>.
- debug-cuda-crash: Call this skill when you need to debug CUDA crashes in SGLang using kernel API logging
- debug-distributed-hang: Debug hanging issues in SGLang distributed inference (TP/PP/DP/EP). Covers identifying hang locations via py-spy/watchdog/cuda coredump, per-rank logging to find state divergence, binary-search methodology for locating the first diverge point, and fix patterns. Use when a multi-GPU SGLang run hangs, freezes, or times out during collective operations.
- env-var-conventions: Conventions for SGLang environment variables — where to define, how to access, how to name, and how to deprecate. Use when adding, renaming, or reviewing any `SGLANG_*` environment variable (or migrating a legacy `SGL_*` alias), or when touching `python/sglang/srt/environ.py`.
- generate-profile: Generate an e2e profiling trace of an SGLang server run. Launches a server, validates accuracy, captures a Chrome-compatible trace, and returns the profile path.
- large-class-style: Code style for SGLang large classes `Scheduler`, `TokenizerManager`, and `ModelRunner`: frozen-code conventions and `__init__` orchestration style. Use when modifying any of these three classes or reviewing changes to them.
- llm-torch-profiler-analysis: Unified LLM torch-profiler triage skill for `sglang`, `vllm`, `TensorRT-LLM`, and `TokenSpeed`. Use it to inspect an existing `trace.json(.gz)` or profile directory, or to drive live profiling against a running server when supported and return one three-table report with kernel, overlap-opportunity, and fuse-pattern tables.
- mechanical-refactor-verify: Make mechanical refactoring (file splits, function moves, module extractions, renames) machine-checkable instead of eyeballed. Reproduce a relocation commit byte-for-byte from faithful primitives, and split an extraction into a verifiable prepare + move + postpare. Use when doing or reviewing such changes.
- scripted-runtime-notes: Requirements for the SGLang scripted runtime, chiefly when to add (vs not add) a harness API. Use for anything related to the scripted runtime.
- sglang-bisect-ci-regression: Investigate consistently failing SGLang CI tests by extracting the failure signature from scheduled or rerun workflows, bisecting the passing/failing commit window, checking runner or hardware specificity, and optionally reproducing on a remote GPU host.
- sglang-cherrypick: Trigger the bot-cherry-pick workflow for a batch of merged PRs onto a release branch and monitor each run to completion. Use when an SGLang release manager asks to cherry-pick a list of PRs to a release branch.
- sglang-prod-incident-triage: Replay-first debug flow for SGLang serving problems. Use when a live or recent server shows health-check failures, latency or throughput regressions, queue growth, timeouts, distributed stalls, crash dumps, wrong outputs after deploys, or PD/EP/HiCache issues, and the job is to turn the problem into a replay plus the right next debug tool.
- sglang-runtime-context: How SGLang's runtime configuration and process-global state are organized (RuntimeContext tiers, resolve-at-end ServerArgs, override entry point, resource/stream/buffer leases, per-forward flags), the CI guardrails that enforce the design, and the idioms for developing and testing against it. Load this before touching server_args, model overrides, module-level state, or per-forward state in sglang.
- speculative-naming: Naming conventions for SGLang speculative decoding identifiers. Use when adding, renaming, or reviewing identifiers in speculative decoding code — anything under `python/sglang/srt/speculative/`, related attention backends, scheduler accumulators, IPC fields, observability metrics, or CLI flags.
- write-sglang-test: Guide for writing SGLang CI/UT tests. Covers CustomTestCase, CI registration, server fixtures, model selection, mock testing, and test placement. Always read test/README.md for the full CI layout, how to run tests, and extra tips. Use when creating new tests, adding CI test cases, writing unit tests, or when the user asks to add tests for SGLang features.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).

First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

[package]
name = "sglang-grpc"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
description = "In-process Rust gRPC server for SGLang"
license = "Apache-2.0"

[lib]
name = "_core"
crate-type = ["cdylib"]

[dependencies]
pyo3 = { version = "0.23", features = ["extension-module"] }
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tonic = { version = "0.12", features = ["gzip", "transport"] }
prost = "0.13"
uuid = { version = "1", features = ["v4"] }
tracing = "0.1"
tracing-subscriber = { version = "0.3", features = ["env-filter"] }
serde_json = "1"
tokenizers = { version = "0.21", default-features = false, features = ["onig"] }
tokio-stream = { version = "0.1", features = ["net"] }
async-stream = "0.3"

[build-dependencies]
tonic-build = "0.12"

[features]
default = ["pyo3/extension-module"]

[profile.release]
opt-level = 2
lto = "thin"
strip = true

[profile.dev]
opt-level = 0
debug = 1

1	fn main() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
2	    let proto_path = "../../proto/sglang/runtime/v1/sglang.proto";
3	
4	    tonic_build::configure()
5	        .build_server(true)
6	        .build_client(false)
7	        .protoc_arg("--experimental_allow_proto3_optional")
8	        .file_descriptor_set_path(
9	            std::path::PathBuf::from(std::env::var("OUT_DIR").unwrap())
10	                .join("sglang_descriptor.bin"),
11	        )
12	        .compile_protos(&[proto_path], &["../../proto"])?;
13	
14	    println!("cargo:rerun-if-changed={}", proto_path);
15	    Ok(())
16	}

"""
Thin gRPC server wrapper — delegates to smg-grpc-servicer package.

A lightweight HTTP sidecar is started alongside the gRPC server to expose:
- /metrics (Prometheus, when --enable-metrics is set)
- /start_profile, /stop_profile (profiling control)

The sidecar is started on --smg-http-sidecar-port (default: --port + 1)
once the gRPC request manager is ready, regardless of whether --enable-metrics
is set.
"""

import inspect
import json
import logging
import time

from aiohttp import web

from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import ProfileReq, ProfileReqType
from sglang.srt.utils.common import get_bool_env_var

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


async def _start_sidecar_server(host: str, port: int, app):
    """Start the aiohttp sidecar and return the runner for cleanup."""
    runner = web.AppRunner(app)
    await runner.setup()
    try:
        site = web.TCPSite(runner, host, port)
        await site.start()
    except BaseException:
        await runner.cleanup()
        raise
    logger.info("HTTP sidecar server started on http://%s:%d", host, port)
    return runner


def _add_metrics_routes(app):
    """Add Prometheus /metrics endpoint to the aiohttp app."""
    from prometheus_client import (
        CollectorRegistry,
        multiprocess,
    )
    from prometheus_client.openmetrics.exposition import (
        CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST,
        generate_latest,
    )

    async def metrics_handler(request):
        try:
            registry = CollectorRegistry()
            multiprocess.MultiProcessCollector(registry)
            data = generate_latest(registry)
            return web.Response(
                body=data,
                headers={"Content-Type": CONTENT_TYPE_LATEST},
            )
        except Exception:
            logger.exception("Failed to generate Prometheus metrics")
            return web.Response(status=500, text="Failed to generate metrics")

    app.router.add_get("/metrics", metrics_handler)


def _check_communicator_results(results, action):
    """Return a web.Response error if results indicate failure, else None."""
    if not results:
        return web.Response(status=500, text="No response from scheduler\n")
    failures = [r for r in results if not r.success]
    if failures:
        msgs = " | ".join(r.message for r in failures)
        return web.Response(status=500, text=f"{action} failed: {msgs}\n")
    return None


def _add_admin_routes(app, request_manager):
    """Add admin endpoints to the aiohttp app.

    Endpoints: /start_profile, /stop_profile.
    Business logic (request construction, env var handling, response interpretation)
    lives here; request_manager only provides the transport to the scheduler.
    """

    async def start_profile_handler(request):
        try:
            if request.content_length and request.content_length > 0:
                try:
                    body = await request.json()
                except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
                    return web.Response(
                        status=400,
                        text=f"Invalid JSON in request body: {e}",
                    )
            else:
                body = {}

            # Build ProfileReq with env var overrides (same as tokenizer_communicator_mixin)
            with_stack = body.get("with_stack")
            env_with_stack = get_bool_env_var("SGLANG_PROFILE_WITH_STACK", "true")
            with_stack = (with_stack is not False) and env_with_stack
            record_shapes = body.get("record_shapes")
            env_record_shapes = get_bool_env_var("SGLANG_PROFILE_RECORD_SHAPES", "true")
            record_shapes = (record_shapes is not False) and env_record_shapes

            req = ProfileReq(
                req_type=ProfileReqType.START_PROFILE,
                output_dir=body.get("output_dir"),
                start_step=body.get("start_step"),
                num_steps=body.get("num_steps"),
                activities=body.get("activities"),
                with_stack=with_stack,
                record_shapes=record_shapes,
                profile_by_stage=body.get("profile_by_stage", False),
                profile_id=str(time.time()),
                merge_profiles=body.get("merge_profiles", False),
                profile_prefix=body.get("profile_prefix"),
                profile_stages=body.get("profile_stages"),
            )
            results = await request_manager.send_communicator_req(
                req, "profile_communicator", timeout=600.0
            )
            err = _check_communicator_results(results, "Start Profile")
            if err:
                return err
            return web.Response(text="Start profiling.\n")
        except Exception as e:
            logger.exception("Failed to start profile")
            return web.Response(
                status=500,
                text=f"Internal error: {type(e).__name__}. Check server logs.\n",
            )

    async def stop_profile_handler(request):
        try:
            req = ProfileReq(req_type=ProfileReqType.STOP_PROFILE)
            results = await request_manager.send_communicator_req(
                req, "profile_communicator", timeout=600.0
            )
            err = _check_communicator_results(results, "Stop profile")
            if err:
                return err
            return web.Response(text="Stop profiling. This will take some time.\n")
        except Exception as e:
            logger.exception("Failed to stop profile")
            return web.Response(
                status=500,
                text=f"Internal error: {type(e).__name__}. Check server logs.\n",
            )

    app.router.add_post("/start_profile", start_profile_handler)
    app.router.add_post("/stop_profile", stop_profile_handler)


async def serve_grpc(server_args, model_info=None):
    """Start the standalone gRPC server with integrated scheduler."""
    try:
        from smg_grpc_servicer.sglang.server import serve_grpc as _serve_grpc
    except ImportError as e:
        raise ImportError(
            "gRPC mode requires the smg-grpc-servicer package. "
            "If not installed, run: pip install smg-grpc-servicer[sglang]. "
            "If already installed, there may be a broken import due to a "
            "version mismatch — see the chained exception above for details."
        ) from e

    sidecar_app = web.Application()
    sidecar_runner = None
    sidecar_port = (
        server_args.smg_http_sidecar_port
        if server_args.smg_http_sidecar_port is not None
        else server_args.port + 1
    )

    # Metrics setup: must set PROMETHEUS_MULTIPROC_DIR before scheduler
    # processes import prometheus_client, since the env var is inherited
    # at fork time.
    if server_args.enable_metrics:
        try:
            from sglang.srt.observability.func_timer import enable_func_timer
            from sglang.srt.utils import set_prometheus_multiproc_dir

            set_prometheus_multiproc_dir()
            enable_func_timer()
            _add_metrics_routes(sidecar_app)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(
                "Failed to set up metrics: %s. Continuing without metrics.",
                e,
                exc_info=True,
            )

    async def _on_request_manager_ready(request_manager, srv_args, sched_info):
        nonlocal sidecar_runner
        try:
            _add_admin_routes(sidecar_app, request_manager)
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(
                "Failed to set up admin routes: %s. "
                "Continuing without admin endpoints.",
                e,
                exc_info=True,
            )
        try:
            sidecar_runner = await _start_sidecar_server(
                server_args.host, sidecar_port, sidecar_app
            )
        except OSError as e:
            logger.error(
                "Failed to start HTTP sidecar server: %s. "
                "Continuing without metrics/profile endpoints.",
                e,
                exc_info=True,
            )
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(
                "Unexpected error starting HTTP sidecar server: %s. "
                "Continuing without metrics/profile endpoints.",
                e,
                exc_info=True,
            )

    # Older smg-grpc-servicer releases (≤ 0.5.2) accept only (server_args,
    # model_info) and reject the on_request_manager_ready hook. The hook is
    # what calls _start_sidecar_server, so dropping the kwarg disables the
    # entire HTTP sidecar (Prometheus /metrics and /start_profile +
    # /stop_profile). Core gRPC serving still works without it.
    serve_kwargs: dict = {}
    sidecar_supported = (
        "on_request_manager_ready" in inspect.signature(_serve_grpc).parameters
    )
    if sidecar_supported:
        serve_kwargs["on_request_manager_ready"] = _on_request_manager_ready
    elif server_args.enable_metrics:
        # User explicitly asked for metrics but the installed servicer can't
        # start the sidecar that serves them — fail loud rather than silently
        # produce a server with no /metrics endpoint.
        raise RuntimeError(
            "--enable-metrics requires smg-grpc-servicer ≥ 0.5.3 (the version "
            "that accepts 'on_request_manager_ready'); installed version "
            "lacks the hook so the HTTP sidecar would never start. Upgrade "
            "smg-grpc-servicer or remove --enable-metrics."
        )
    else:
        logger.warning(
            "Installed smg-grpc-servicer does not accept "
            "'on_request_manager_ready'; HTTP sidecar disabled "
            "(no /metrics, /start_profile, /stop_profile). "
            "Upgrade smg-grpc-servicer to ≥ 0.5.3 to enable it."
        )

    try:
        await _serve_grpc(server_args, model_info, **serve_kwargs)
    finally:
        if sidecar_runner is not None:
            try:
                await sidecar_runner.cleanup()
            except Exception as e:
                logger.exception(
                    "Failed to cleanly shut down HTTP sidecar server: %s",
                    e,
                )

1	"""sglang build hooks.
2	
3	SGLANG_BUILD_RUST_EXTS controls which Rust extensions are built:
4	  - unset or "all": build every declared Rust extension (the default).
5	  - "none": build no Rust extensions.
6	  - comma-separated names: build only extensions whose target matches one of the
7	    given (case-insensitive) substrings, e.g. "grpc" matches
8	    "sglang.srt.grpc._core".
9	
10	This is a build-time environment variable, so it is read directly from
11	os.environ instead of sglang.srt.environ, which is not available until after the
12	package has been built.
13	"""
14	
15	import os
16	
17	from setuptools import setup

## Project Summary

**Project**: `sglang-grpc` — an in-process Rust gRPC server for SGLang. It's a PyO3 native extension (`sglang.srt.grpc._core`) that provides a high-performance gRPC front-end to SGLang's Python runtime.

### Architecture:
1. **lib.rs** — PyO3 module entry. Exposes `start_server()`, `GrpcServerHandle`, `ChunkSendStatus`. Sets up Tokio runtime in a background thread, extracts tokenizer info from the Python `RuntimeHandle`, optionally loads a Rust-native tokenizer, builds the `PyBridge`, and runs the Tonic gRPC server.

2. **build.rs** — Uses `tonic-build` to compile the proto (`proto/sglang/runtime/v1/sglang.proto`) into Rust server stubs. Generates file descriptor set.

3. **server.rs** — Implements `SglangService` (the gRPC service trait from proto). Handles:
   - SGLang-native RPCs: TextGenerate/Generate (streaming), TextEmbed/Embed/Classify (unary), Tokenize/Detokenize (Rust-native with Python fallback), HealthCheck, GetModelInfo, GetServerInfo, ListModels, GetLoad, Abort, FlushCache, Pause/ContinueGeneration.
   - OpenAI-compatible RPCs (JSON pass-through): ChatComplete, Complete (streaming), OpenAIEmbed, OpenAIClassify, Score, Rerank (unary).
   - Admin RPCs: StartProfile, StopProfile, UpdateWeightsFromDisk.
   - Streaming via `async_stream`, abort guards (`RequestAbortGuard` with Drop semantics), response timeouts, terminal error mapping.
   - `run_grpc_server()` — Tonic server bootstrap with max message size, graceful shutdown.

4. **bridge.rs** — `PyBridge`: holds the Python `RuntimeHandle` and per-request channels. Manages `BridgeState` (channels, pending_sends, ready_callbacks, ready_signals, terminal_errors) behind a `Mutex`. Submits requests to Python via GIL, with `ChunkCallback` / `JsonChunkCallback` (PyO3 classes) that Python calls to push response chunks. Backpressure handling via `try_send` + pending-send parking + `on_ready` signaling. Terminal errors: `ChannelFull`, `ClientDisconnected`, `Aborted`.

5. **tokenizers.rs** — `RustTokenizer` with pluggable backend trait (`TokenizerBackend`). Currently only `HuggingFaceTokenizerBackend` (uses the `tokenizers` crate). Falls back to Python for unsupported (e.g., `tokenizer_mode=slow`).

6. **utils/** — 
   - `request_utils.rs` — builds serde_json request dicts from proto messages (SamplingParams, DisaggregatedParams, trace headers, etc.) to pass to Python's `GenerateReqInput`/`EmbeddingReqInput`.
   - `py_utils.rs` — JSON↔Python conversions (`json_map_to_pydict`, `py_value_to_json_string`).
   - `mod.rs` — re-exports.

7. **tests** — unit tests in `server/tests.rs`, `bridge/tests.rs`, `py_utils/tests.rs`, `request_utils.rs`.

- PyO3 native extension model (cdylib, `_core` module, `#[pyfunction]`, `#[pyclass]`)

- Tonic gRPC server: proto compilation, service trait impl, streaming vs unary
- The bridge channel architecture: mpsc channels per request, callbacks, backpressure, terminal errors
- Abort propagation: `RequestAbortGuard` Drop semantics, `spawn_blocking`, `abort_all`
- Tokenizer native acceleration + fallback
- Request dict building: proto → serde_json → PyDict
- OpenAI JSON pass-through design
- Error mapping: PyErr → tonic Status, terminal errors → gRPC codes

input.rationale: 本手册围绕 sglang-grpc 这一用 Rust+PyO3 编写的 SGLang 进程内原生 gRPC 服务扩展展开。该 crate 体量虽小，但横跨 PyO3 扩展、Tonic gRPC、Tokio 异步运行时与 Python GIL 互操作四个领域，且在背压、中止传播、错误映射方面内容较深，因此采用三层结构：入门层(u1)先让读者建立项目定位、构建链、目录地图与启动入口的整体认知，确保从零可理解、可构建；进阶层(u2)按"proto 契约→服务实现总览→流式分发→一元分发→Python 桥接→回调→请求字典构建→原生分词器"的真实调用链顺序逐层展开核心模块，使每篇只讲一个相对独立的机制；专家层(u3)聚焦背压停泊、RAII 中止传播、Python 异常到 gRPC 状态码映射、服务引导与优雅关停、Tokio/GIL 协作模型以及测试与扩展实践。讲义之间通过 depends_on 显式标注依赖，遵循"先整体后细节、先数据流后并发控制"的递进，避免读者在未理解桥接通道前就接触背压等高阶话题。所有 source_files 均为已确认存在的真实文件，full 模式下 action 全部为 new。

units.title: 入门：项目定位、构建与启动入口

lectures.title: 项目定位与整体架构：Rust 原生 gRPC 服务扩展
lectures.topic: 介绍 sglang-grpc 是什么——一个用 Rust+PyO3 编写、编译为 Python 扩展 sglang.srt.grpc._core 的进程内原生 gRPC 服务，以及它与 SGLang Python RuntimeHandle、外部 smg-grpc-servicer 包之间的边界关系。
learning_goals: 理解项目定位：为 SGLang 提供高性能原生 gRPC 前端
理解 PyO3 扩展模型：cdylib crate 编译为可被 import 的 _core 模块
理解 Rust 服务与 Python 运行时(RuntimeHandle)的职责边界
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 Cargo.toml 的 [lib] 段，说明 crate-type=["cdylib"] 与 name="_core" 如何对应到 Python 端的 sglang.srt.grpc._core；再在 python/pyproject.toml 中找到对应的 [[tool.setuptools-rust.ext-modules]] 声明，写一段文字解释 target/path/binding 三个字段如何把本 crate 接入 Python 包构建。

lectures.title: 构建链与工具链：Cargo、build.rs 与 setuptools-rust
lectures.topic: 讲解构建链：Cargo.toml 的依赖与 profile、build.rs 用 tonic-build 编译 proto、rust-toolchain.toml 固定工具链，以及 Python 端 setup.py 的 BuildRust 如何通过 SGLANG_BUILD_RUST_EXTS 选择性构建本扩展。
learning_goals: 理解 build.rs 用 tonic_build::configure 编译 proto 的流程与产物(file_descriptor_set)
理解关键依赖(tonic/prost/pyo3/tokio/tokenizers)的作用
理解 setuptools-rust 打包与 SGLANG_BUILD_RUST_EXTS 的子串匹配选择机制
lectures.practice_task: 在 build.rs 中找到 cargo:rerun-if-changed 指令，说明它为何只盯住 proto 文件；然后阅读 python/setup.py 的 _selected_rust_extensions，说明当设置 SGLANG_BUILD_RUST_EXTS=grpc 时为何能只构建 sglang.srt.grpc._core 而跳过 sglang-mm。

lectures.title: 目录结构与模块地图
lectures.topic: 梳理 src 目录结构：lib/server/bridge/tokenizers/utils 各模块的职责，proto 子模块的引入方式，以及内嵌测试文件(server/tests.rs、bridge/tests.rs 等)的组织约定。
learning_goals: 能画出四个业务模块(bridge/server/tokenizers/utils)与 proto 模块的依赖关系
理解 pub mod 与 pub(crate) mod 的可见性差异(utils 为何是 crate 私有)
知道每个源文件大致负责什么，便于后续精读定位
minimal_modules: lib.rs 模块声明(pub mod / pub(crate) mod)
lectures.practice_task: 根据 src/lib.rs 顶部的模块声明，画出 bridge/server/tokenizers/utils/proto 之间的依赖图；指出 utils 模块为何声明为 pub(crate) 而不是 pub，并说明这种可见性对作为 Python 扩展的 crate 有何意义。

lectures.title: 启动入口全景：start_server 与 GrpcServerHandle 生命周期
lectures.topic: 逐段讲解 lib.rs 的 #[pyfunction] start_server：地址解析与绑定、提取 tokenizer 信息、参数归一化、构建多线程 Tokio 运行时、构造 PyBridge、在独立 OS 线程启动 gRPC 服务，以及 GrpcServerHandle 的 shutdown/is_alive 控制。
learning_goals: 理解服务启动的完整顺序与每一步的副作用
理解 worker_threads/response_channel_capacity/response_timeout_secs 的下限归一化
理解 GrpcServerHandle 通过 Notify 与 JoinHandle 控制服务生命周期的语义
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 start_server 的 #[pyo3(signature = ...)]，列出全部参数及其默认值；并解释为什么先用标准库 TcpListener 绑定地址、调用 set_nonblocking(true)，再把 listener 转交 Tokio(提示：与端口占用错误时机和 from_std 有关)。

units.title: 进阶：proto 契约、服务实现与 Python 桥接

lectures.title: proto 契约与 tonic 代码生成
lectures.topic: 讲解 proto/sglang/runtime/v1/sglang.proto 定义的服务与消息(SGLang 原生 RPC、OpenAI 透传、Admin 三类)，以及 tonic-build 如何生成 Rust 服务 trait 与消息结构，再由 tonic::include_proto! 导入。
learning_goals: 读懂 proto 服务的三类 RPC 与 stream 返回关键字
理解 build.rs 的 tonic_build::configure 如何控制生成 server 端代码
理解 include_proto!("sglang.runtime.v1") 与 package 名的对应关系
lectures.practice_task: 在 proto 文件中统计 rpc 定义的总数并按三类(原生/OpenAI 透传/Admin)分组；然后说明若要新增一个最简单的一元 RPC，需要在 proto、build.rs、server.rs 三处分别做哪些改动(build.rs 是否需要手改)。

lectures.title: 服务实现总览：SglangServiceImpl 与三类 RPC
lectures.topic: 总览 server.rs 中 SglangServiceImpl 对 SglangService trait 的实现布局：bridge 与 response_timeout 字段、流式 RPC 用关联 type XStream 声明、一元 RPC 直接返回 Response，以及 OpenAI 透传与 Admin/Ops RPC 的组织。
learning_goals: 理解 #[tonic::async_trait] 实现的整体结构与字段
区分流式(stream)与一元(unary) RPC 的返回类型模式
认识三类 RPC 的公共提交模式(submit_request / submit_json / submit_openai)
lectures.practice_task: 在 server.rs 中统计 SglangService impl 块实现了多少个 async fn，并按 SGLang 原生 / OpenAI 透传 / Admin 三类分组列出；指出流式 RPC 与一元 RPC 各自的返回 Result<Response<...>> 内层类型差异。

lectures.title: 流式 RPC 与 async_stream：text_generate / generate / OpenAI 流
lectures.topic: 深入流式 RPC 的实现：async_stream::stream! 宏构造响应流、对 ResponseChunk 的 Data/Finished/Error/None 四分支匹配循环、finished 标志的设置，以及与 RequestAbortGuard 的集成。
learning_goals: 掌握用 async_stream::stream! 构造 tonic 响应流的写法
理解 ResponseChunk::Data/Finished/Error/None 四种分支各自的产出与终止语义
理解 rid 的自动生成(uuid)与在闭包中的 clone 必要性
lectures.practice_task: 对照 text_generate 的 stream! 循环，说明每收到一个 ResponseChunk::Data 时 yield 的 TextGenerateResponse 中 finished 为何是 false；并解释 Ok(None) 分支如何借助 closed_stream_status 决定是否调用 abort_guard.abort_now()。

lectures.title: 一元 RPC 与 JSON 响应解析：embed/classify/list_models 等
lectures.topic: 讲解一元 RPC(text_embed/embed/classify/list_models/flush_cache 等)如何用 recv_terminal_chunk_for_request/recv_json_response 收取单个终止 chunk，并用 serde_json 解析 Python 返回的 JSON 字符串、做默认值兜底。
learning_goals: 理解一元响应只期望 Finished 终止 chunk(收到 Data 视为协议违例)
掌握把 Python JSON 字符串解析为 proto 响应字段并兜底默认值的模式
理解 classify 复用 embed 提交路径(req_type="embed")的原因
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 list_models，画出从 JSON 数组到 proto::ModelCard 的字段映射表(id/root/parent/max_model_len 的取值与兜底)；并说明 classify 在调用 submit_request 时为何传 "embed" 作为 req_type(提示：proto 注释与内部 EmbeddingReqInput 路径)。

lectures.title: PyBridge 与请求通道架构
lectures.topic: 讲解 bridge.rs 的 PyBridge：持有 Python RuntimeHandle 与 per-request mpsc 通道，BridgeState(channels/pending_sends/ready_callbacks/ready_signals/terminal_errors) 由 Mutex 保护；create_channel 做去重，submit_request 通过 GIL 调用 Python submit_request。
learning_goals: 理解桥接的核心数据结构 PyBridge 与 BridgeState 及其 Mutex 保护
理解 rid 到 mpsc Sender 的映射与重复 rid 的报错处理
理解 Python 方法调用(kwargs 构造)与失败时 remove_channel 的清理
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 create_channel，说明遇到重复 rid 时返回何种错误、并清理了哪些与该 rid 相关的旧状态(terminal_errors/ready_callbacks/...);再解释 submit_request 在 Python 调用失败时为何要调用 remove_channel(rid)。

lectures.title: 回调机制：ChunkCallback 与 JsonChunkCallback
lectures.topic: 讲解两个 #[pyclass] 回调：Python 通过 __call__ 推送 chunk，Rust 从中提取 text/output_ids/embedding/json_bytes 与 meta_info；以及 set_on_ready/clear_on_ready 的就绪信号机制和 meta_info 的 JSON 编码。
learning_goals: 理解 Python→Rust 的回调数据流与 ResponseData 字段提取
理解 finished/error 分支如何决定 ResponseChunk 变体
理解为何 meta_info 每个值都要 JSON 编码以适配 proto map<string,string>
lectures.practice_task: 对照 ChunkCallback.__call__ 与 JsonChunkCallback.__call__，列表说明二者在提取 ResponseData 字段(text/embedding/json_bytes/meta_info)上的差异；并解释 extract_meta_info 为何调用 py_value_to_json_string 把每个 value(包括字符串)做 JSON 编码。

lectures.title: 请求字典构建：proto 消息到 Python dict
lectures.topic: 讲解 utils/request_utils.rs 的 build_*_dict 函数：把 proto 消息转成 serde_json map 再经 py_utils 的 json_map_to_pydict 交给 Python；涵盖 SamplingParams、DisaggregatedParams、trace_headers、optional 字段的条件插入。
learning_goals: 掌握 proto→serde_json→PyDict 的字段映射流程
理解 optional/repeated 字段的条件与整体插入策略
理解 py_utils 的 json_map_to_pydict 与 py_value_to_json_string 两个转换的作用
lectures.practice_task: 对比 build_text_generate_dict 与 build_generate_dict，列出二者共有字段与各自独有字段(如 text vs input_ids、return_text_in_logprobs);并说明 sampling_params_to_map 如何处理 stop / stop_token_ids 这类 repeated 字段(为何用 !p.stop.is_empty() 判断)。

lectures.title: Rust 原生分词器与 Python 回退
lectures.topic: 讲解 tokenizers.rs 的 RustTokenizer 与可插拔后端 trait TokenizerBackend(目前仅 HuggingFaceTokenizerBackend)，以及 server.rs 中 tokenize/detokenize 的"先 Rust(无 GIL)后 Python(spawn_blocking)"回退策略。
learning_goals: 理解后端抽象 TokenizerBackend trait 与 load_backend 的扩展点
理解 from_tokenizer_path 在哪些情况下返回 None(禁用 Rust 分词器)
理解 server.rs 中 tokenize/detokenize 的原生优先与 Python 回退分支
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 from_tokenizer_path，列出所有会让 Rust 分词器返回 None 的情况(无 tokenizer.json、tokenizer_mode=slow、后端加载失败);再在 server.rs 的 tokenize 中追踪：当 self.bridge.rust_tokenizer() 为 Some 时走原生路径返回什么，为 None 时如何用 spawn_blocking 回退到 bridge.tokenize_py 并解析 JSON。

units.title: 专家：并发、背压、错误与生命周期

lectures.title: 背压与 pending-send 停泊机制
lectures.topic: 深入 try_send_chunk 的背压处理：通道满时(TrySendError::Full)注册 pending_send、用 tokio_handle.spawn 异步发送该 chunk、通过 on_ready/ready_signals 边沿通知 Python 恢复生产，以及 ChannelFull/ClientDisconnected 终止错误。
learning_goals: 理解非阻塞 try_send + tokio spawn 兜底的背压设计动机
理解 register_pending_send 防止同 rid 堆积第二个 parked chunk 而直接关闭流
理解终端 chunk(Finished/Error)排空后不再触发 on_ready 的契约
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 try_send_chunk 的 TrySendError::Full 分支，画出"通道满→spawn 异步 sender.send().await→mark_send_ready→notify_ready"的时序图;并说明为何 register_pending_send 返回 false(即已有一个 pending)时会立即 close_channel_with_error(ChannelFull)。

lectures.title: 中止传播：RequestAbortGuard 与 abort/abort_all
lectures.topic: 讲解 server.rs 的 RequestAbortGuard(利用 Drop 在响应流提前结束时向 Python 发起 abort)与 bridge.rs 的 abort/abort_all：批量 drain 通道、写入 TerminalError::Aborted、再调用 Python abort 方法。
learning_goals: 理解 RAII 式 abort：guard 的 disarm/abort_now/drop 三种处置路径
理解 spawn_abort 用 spawn_blocking 避免阻塞 Tokio worker
理解 abort_all 的批量清理语义及其安全告警含义
lectures.practice_task: 对照 RequestAbortGuard 的 drop 与 disarm/abort_now，分别说明三种情形下 guard 如何处置：流正常收到 Finished、客户端断开(Ok(None))、超时(DeadlineExceeded);再解释 bridge.rs 的 abort_all 为何在 server.rs 的 abort RPC 中触发"仅限可信客户端"的告警。

lectures.title: 错误映射：PyErr 到 gRPC Status
lectures.topic: 讲解错误映射体系：pyerr_to_status 把 PyValueError/PyTypeError 映射为 INVALID_ARGUMENT、其余为 INTERNAL；terminal_error_status 把 ChannelFull/Aborted/ClientDisconnected 映射为 RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED/CANCELLED；closed_stream_status 与 recv_chunk_with_timeout 的 DEADLINE_EXCEEDED 超时。
learning_goals: 掌握 Python 异常到 gRPC 状态码的两分法(客户端错误 vs 服务端错误)
掌握 TerminalError 三变体到 gRPC Code 的映射
理解 closed_stream_status 如何区分"已有终端错误"与"流异常关闭需 abort"
lectures.practice_task: 制作一张映射表，把 TerminalError::ChannelFull/ClientDisconnected/Aborted 与对应的 gRPC Code(RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED/CANCELLED)一一对应;并说明 pyerr_to_status 如何用 Python::with_gil + is_instance_of::<PyValueError/PyTypeError> 区分 INVALID_ARGUMENT 与 INTERNAL。

lectures.title: Tonic 服务引导、消息大小上限与优雅关停
lectures.topic: 讲解 run_grpc_server：构建 SglangServiceImpl、用 resolve_max_message_size 解析 SGLANG_TONIC_PAYLOAD 与默认 64MiB 上限、配置 max_decoding/encoding_message_size、serve_with_incoming_shutdown 接收 Notify 信号，并与 GrpcServerHandle.shutdown 协作。
learning_goals: 理解服务引导流程与消息大小上限的意义(高于 tonic 4MiB 默认)
理解 SGLANG_TONIC_PAYLOAD 的合法/非法/0 值处理与回退
理解优雅关停的 shutdown 信号链(Notify→serve_with_incoming_shutdown)
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 resolve_max_message_size，说明当 SGLANG_TONIC_PAYLOAD 设为非数字字符串、0、合法正数时的不同行为;再从 GrpcServerHandle::shutdown 的 notify_one() 出发，追踪信号如何传递到 serve_with_incoming_shutdown 的异步闭包并最终停止 tonic Server。

lectures.title: Tokio 运行时与 GIL 协作模型
lectures.topic: 讲解并发模型：start_server 在独立 std::thread 里构建多线程 Tokio 运行时并把 handle 交给 PyBridge；Python GIL 调用通过 Python::with_gil / tokio::task::spawn_blocking 完成；回调持有 tokio_handle 以便在通道满时 spawn 异步发送。
learning_goals: 理解 Tokio 运行时与承载它的 OS 线程的关系及为何独占一个线程
理解 GIL 获取时机与为何用 spawn_blocking 包装同步 GIL 调用
理解 PyBridge / 回调为何需要保存并共享 tokio_handle
minimal_modules: tokio_handle 的 clone 与共享
server.rs 中的 spawn_blocking 调用点
lectures.practice_task: 在 server.rs 中找出所有 tokio::task::spawn_blocking 调用(如 health_check/get_model_info/tokenize 的 Python 回退),说明它们为何不能直接在 async fn 里用 Python::with_gil 同步阻塞;再解释 PyBridge 构造时保存 tokio_handle 的用途(提示：与 try_send_chunk 的 Full 分支有关)。

lectures.title: 测试组织与扩展实践：新增 RPC、新增分词器后端
lectures.topic: 讲解单元测试组织(server/tests.rs、bridge/tests.rs、py_utils/tests.rs、request_utils 内联测试)与串行 env 测试模式；并以两个扩展场景收尾——如何新增一个 RPC、如何新增一个 TokenizerBackend。
learning_goals: 掌握现有测试写法与 SGLANG_TONIC_PAYLOAD 这类 env 串行测试的 SAFETY 约定
理解新增一个 gRPC RPC 需要改动的完整清单(proto、server trait 实现、请求字典、错误映射)
理解 tokenizers.rs 的 load_backend 后端扩展点
lectures.practice_task: 参照 request_utils.rs 内联测试(generate_dicts_include_session_id 等)的风格，为 build_text_embed_dict 编写一个单元测试，断言 routing_key 与 trace_headers 被正确写入字典、且空 trace_headers 时不出现 external_trace_header 键;或参照 HuggingFaceTokenizerBackend 为 TokenizerBackend 新增一个占位后端结构体并在 load_backend 中注册探测。

units.title: 入门：项目定位、构建与启动入口

lectures.title: 项目定位与整体架构：Rust 原生 gRPC 服务扩展
lectures.topic: 介绍 sglang-grpc 是什么——一个用 Rust+PyO3 编写、编译为 Python 扩展 sglang.srt.grpc._core 的进程内原生 gRPC 服务，以及它与 SGLang Python RuntimeHandle、外部 smg-grpc-servicer 包之间的边界关系。
learning_goals: 理解项目定位：为 SGLang 提供高性能原生 gRPC 前端
理解 PyO3 扩展模型：cdylib crate 编译为可被 import 的 _core 模块
理解 Rust 服务与 Python 运行时(RuntimeHandle)的职责边界
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 Cargo.toml 的 [lib] 段，说明 crate-type=["cdylib"] 与 name="_core" 如何对应到 Python 端的 sglang.srt.grpc._core；再在 python/pyproject.toml 中找到对应的 [[tool.setuptools-rust.ext-modules]] 声明，写一段文字解释 target/path/binding 三个字段如何把本 crate 接入 Python 包构建。

lectures.title: 构建链与工具链：Cargo、build.rs 与 setuptools-rust
lectures.topic: 讲解构建链：Cargo.toml 的依赖与 profile、build.rs 用 tonic-build 编译 proto、rust-toolchain.toml 固定工具链，以及 Python 端 setup.py 的 BuildRust 如何通过 SGLANG_BUILD_RUST_EXTS 选择性构建本扩展。
learning_goals: 理解 build.rs 用 tonic_build::configure 编译 proto 的流程与产物(file_descriptor_set)
理解关键依赖(tonic/prost/pyo3/tokio/tokenizers)的作用
理解 setuptools-rust 打包与 SGLANG_BUILD_RUST_EXTS 的子串匹配选择机制
lectures.practice_task: 在 build.rs 中找到 cargo:rerun-if-changed 指令，说明它为何只盯住 proto 文件；然后阅读 python/setup.py 的 _selected_rust_extensions，说明当设置 SGLANG_BUILD_RUST_EXTS=grpc 时为何能只构建 sglang.srt.grpc._core 而跳过 sglang-mm。

lectures.title: 目录结构与模块地图
lectures.topic: 梳理 src 目录结构：lib/server/bridge/tokenizers/utils 各模块的职责，proto 子模块的引入方式，以及内嵌测试文件(server/tests.rs、bridge/tests.rs 等)的组织约定。
learning_goals: 能画出四个业务模块(bridge/server/tokenizers/utils)与 proto 模块的依赖关系
理解 pub mod 与 pub(crate) mod 的可见性差异(utils 为何是 crate 私有)
知道每个源文件大致负责什么，便于后续精读定位
minimal_modules: lib.rs 模块声明(pub mod / pub(crate) mod)
lectures.practice_task: 根据 src/lib.rs 顶部的模块声明，画出 bridge/server/tokenizers/utils/proto 之间的依赖图；指出 utils 模块为何声明为 pub(crate) 而不是 pub，并说明这种可见性对作为 Python 扩展的 crate 有何意义。

lectures.title: 启动入口全景：start_server 与 GrpcServerHandle 生命周期
lectures.topic: 逐段讲解 lib.rs 的 #[pyfunction] start_server：地址解析与绑定、提取 tokenizer 信息、参数归一化、构建多线程 Tokio 运行时、构造 PyBridge、在独立 OS 线程启动 gRPC 服务，以及 GrpcServerHandle 的 shutdown/is_alive 控制。
learning_goals: 理解服务启动的完整顺序与每一步的副作用
理解 worker_threads/response_channel_capacity/response_timeout_secs 的下限归一化
理解 GrpcServerHandle 通过 Notify 与 JoinHandle 控制服务生命周期的语义
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 start_server 的 #[pyo3(signature = ...)]，列出全部参数及其默认值；并解释为什么先用标准库 TcpListener 绑定地址、调用 set_nonblocking(true)，再把 listener 转交 Tokio(提示：与端口占用错误时机和 from_std 有关)。

units.title: 进阶：proto 契约、服务实现与 Python 桥接

lectures.title: proto 契约与 tonic 代码生成
lectures.topic: 讲解 proto/sglang/runtime/v1/sglang.proto 定义的服务与消息(SGLang 原生 RPC、OpenAI 透传、Admin 三类)，以及 tonic-build 如何生成 Rust 服务 trait 与消息结构，再由 tonic::include_proto! 导入。
learning_goals: 读懂 proto 服务的三类 RPC 与 stream 返回关键字
理解 build.rs 的 tonic_build::configure 如何控制生成 server 端代码
理解 include_proto!("sglang.runtime.v1") 与 package 名的对应关系
lectures.practice_task: 在 proto 文件中统计 rpc 定义的总数并按三类(原生/OpenAI 透传/Admin)分组；然后说明若要新增一个最简单的一元 RPC，需要在 proto、build.rs、server.rs 三处分别做哪些改动(build.rs 是否需要手改)。

lectures.title: 服务实现总览：SglangServiceImpl 与三类 RPC
lectures.topic: 总览 server.rs 中 SglangServiceImpl 对 SglangService trait 的实现布局：bridge 与 response_timeout 字段、流式 RPC 用关联 type XStream 声明、一元 RPC 直接返回 Response，以及 OpenAI 透传与 Admin/Ops RPC 的组织。
learning_goals: 理解 #[tonic::async_trait] 实现的整体结构与字段
区分流式(stream)与一元(unary) RPC 的返回类型模式
认识三类 RPC 的公共提交模式(submit_request / submit_json / submit_openai)
lectures.practice_task: 在 server.rs 中统计 SglangService impl 块实现了多少个 async fn，并按 SGLang 原生 / OpenAI 透传 / Admin 三类分组列出；指出流式 RPC 与一元 RPC 各自的返回 Result<Response<...>> 内层类型差异。

lectures.title: 流式 RPC 与 async_stream：text_generate / generate / OpenAI 流
lectures.topic: 深入流式 RPC 的实现：async_stream::stream! 宏构造响应流、对 ResponseChunk 的 Data/Finished/Error/None 四分支匹配循环、finished 标志的设置，以及与 RequestAbortGuard 的集成。
learning_goals: 掌握用 async_stream::stream! 构造 tonic 响应流的写法
理解 ResponseChunk::Data/Finished/Error/None 四种分支各自的产出与终止语义
理解 rid 的自动生成(uuid)与在闭包中的 clone 必要性
lectures.practice_task: 对照 text_generate 的 stream! 循环，说明每收到一个 ResponseChunk::Data 时 yield 的 TextGenerateResponse 中 finished 为何是 false；并解释 Ok(None) 分支如何借助 closed_stream_status 决定是否调用 abort_guard.abort_now()。

lectures.title: 一元 RPC 与 JSON 响应解析：embed/classify/list_models 等
lectures.topic: 讲解一元 RPC(text_embed/embed/classify/list_models/flush_cache 等)如何用 recv_terminal_chunk_for_request/recv_json_response 收取单个终止 chunk，并用 serde_json 解析 Python 返回的 JSON 字符串、做默认值兜底。
learning_goals: 理解一元响应只期望 Finished 终止 chunk(收到 Data 视为协议违例)
掌握把 Python JSON 字符串解析为 proto 响应字段并兜底默认值的模式
理解 classify 复用 embed 提交路径(req_type="embed")的原因
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 list_models，画出从 JSON 数组到 proto::ModelCard 的字段映射表(id/root/parent/max_model_len 的取值与兜底)；并说明 classify 在调用 submit_request 时为何传 "embed" 作为 req_type(提示：proto 注释与内部 EmbeddingReqInput 路径)。

lectures.title: PyBridge 与请求通道架构
lectures.topic: 讲解 bridge.rs 的 PyBridge：持有 Python RuntimeHandle 与 per-request mpsc 通道，BridgeState(channels/pending_sends/ready_callbacks/ready_signals/terminal_errors) 由 Mutex 保护；create_channel 做去重，submit_request 通过 GIL 调用 Python submit_request。
learning_goals: 理解桥接的核心数据结构 PyBridge 与 BridgeState 及其 Mutex 保护
理解 rid 到 mpsc Sender 的映射与重复 rid 的报错处理
理解 Python 方法调用(kwargs 构造)与失败时 remove_channel 的清理
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 create_channel，说明遇到重复 rid 时返回何种错误、并清理了哪些与该 rid 相关的旧状态(terminal_errors/ready_callbacks/...);再解释 submit_request 在 Python 调用失败时为何要调用 remove_channel(rid)。

lectures.title: 回调机制：ChunkCallback 与 JsonChunkCallback
lectures.topic: 讲解两个 #[pyclass] 回调：Python 通过 __call__ 推送 chunk，Rust 从中提取 text/output_ids/embedding/json_bytes 与 meta_info；以及 set_on_ready/clear_on_ready 的就绪信号机制和 meta_info 的 JSON 编码。
learning_goals: 理解 Python→Rust 的回调数据流与 ResponseData 字段提取
理解 finished/error 分支如何决定 ResponseChunk 变体
理解为何 meta_info 每个值都要 JSON 编码以适配 proto map<string,string>
lectures.practice_task: 对照 ChunkCallback.__call__ 与 JsonChunkCallback.__call__，列表说明二者在提取 ResponseData 字段(text/embedding/json_bytes/meta_info)上的差异；并解释 extract_meta_info 为何调用 py_value_to_json_string 把每个 value(包括字符串)做 JSON 编码。

lectures.title: 请求字典构建：proto 消息到 Python dict
lectures.topic: 讲解 utils/request_utils.rs 的 build_*_dict 函数：把 proto 消息转成 serde_json map 再经 py_utils 的 json_map_to_pydict 交给 Python；涵盖 SamplingParams、DisaggregatedParams、trace_headers、optional 字段的条件插入。
learning_goals: 掌握 proto→serde_json→PyDict 的字段映射流程
理解 optional/repeated 字段的条件与整体插入策略
理解 py_utils 的 json_map_to_pydict 与 py_value_to_json_string 两个转换的作用
lectures.practice_task: 对比 build_text_generate_dict 与 build_generate_dict，列出二者共有字段与各自独有字段(如 text vs input_ids、return_text_in_logprobs);并说明 sampling_params_to_map 如何处理 stop / stop_token_ids 这类 repeated 字段(为何用 !p.stop.is_empty() 判断)。

lectures.title: Rust 原生分词器与 Python 回退
lectures.topic: 讲解 tokenizers.rs 的 RustTokenizer 与可插拔后端 trait TokenizerBackend(目前仅 HuggingFaceTokenizerBackend)，以及 server.rs 中 tokenize/detokenize 的"先 Rust(无 GIL)后 Python(spawn_blocking)"回退策略。
learning_goals: 理解后端抽象 TokenizerBackend trait 与 load_backend 的扩展点
理解 from_tokenizer_path 在哪些情况下返回 None(禁用 Rust 分词器)
理解 server.rs 中 tokenize/detokenize 的原生优先与 Python 回退分支
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 from_tokenizer_path，列出所有会让 Rust 分词器返回 None 的情况(无 tokenizer.json、tokenizer_mode=slow、后端加载失败);再在 server.rs 的 tokenize 中追踪：当 self.bridge.rust_tokenizer() 为 Some 时走原生路径返回什么，为 None 时如何用 spawn_blocking 回退到 bridge.tokenize_py 并解析 JSON。

units.title: 专家：并发、背压、错误与生命周期

lectures.title: 背压与 pending-send 停泊机制
lectures.topic: 深入 try_send_chunk 的背压处理：通道满时(TrySendError::Full)注册 pending_send、用 tokio_handle.spawn 异步发送该 chunk、通过 on_ready/ready_signals 边沿通知 Python 恢复生产，以及 ChannelFull/ClientDisconnected 终止错误。
learning_goals: 理解非阻塞 try_send + tokio spawn 兜底的背压设计动机
理解 register_pending_send 防止同 rid 堆积第二个 parked chunk 而直接关闭流
理解终端 chunk(Finished/Error)排空后不再触发 on_ready 的契约
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 try_send_chunk 的 TrySendError::Full 分支，画出"通道满→spawn 异步 sender.send().await→mark_send_ready→notify_ready"的时序图;并说明为何 register_pending_send 返回 false(即已有一个 pending)时会立即 close_channel_with_error(ChannelFull)。

lectures.title: 中止传播：RequestAbortGuard 与 abort/abort_all
lectures.topic: 讲解 server.rs 的 RequestAbortGuard(利用 Drop 在响应流提前结束时向 Python 发起 abort)与 bridge.rs 的 abort/abort_all：批量 drain 通道、写入 TerminalError::Aborted、再调用 Python abort 方法。
learning_goals: 理解 RAII 式 abort：guard 的 disarm/abort_now/drop 三种处置路径
理解 spawn_abort 用 spawn_blocking 避免阻塞 Tokio worker
理解 abort_all 的批量清理语义及其安全告警含义
lectures.practice_task: 对照 RequestAbortGuard 的 drop 与 disarm/abort_now，分别说明三种情形下 guard 如何处置：流正常收到 Finished、客户端断开(Ok(None))、超时(DeadlineExceeded);再解释 bridge.rs 的 abort_all 为何在 server.rs 的 abort RPC 中触发"仅限可信客户端"的告警。

lectures.title: 错误映射：PyErr 到 gRPC Status
lectures.topic: 讲解错误映射体系：pyerr_to_status 把 PyValueError/PyTypeError 映射为 INVALID_ARGUMENT、其余为 INTERNAL；terminal_error_status 把 ChannelFull/Aborted/ClientDisconnected 映射为 RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED/CANCELLED；closed_stream_status 与 recv_chunk_with_timeout 的 DEADLINE_EXCEEDED 超时。
learning_goals: 掌握 Python 异常到 gRPC 状态码的两分法(客户端错误 vs 服务端错误)
掌握 TerminalError 三变体到 gRPC Code 的映射
理解 closed_stream_status 如何区分"已有终端错误"与"流异常关闭需 abort"
lectures.practice_task: 制作一张映射表，把 TerminalError::ChannelFull/ClientDisconnected/Aborted 与对应的 gRPC Code(RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED/CANCELLED)一一对应;并说明 pyerr_to_status 如何用 Python::with_gil + is_instance_of::<PyValueError/PyTypeError> 区分 INVALID_ARGUMENT 与 INTERNAL。

lectures.title: Tonic 服务引导、消息大小上限与优雅关停
lectures.topic: 讲解 run_grpc_server：构建 SglangServiceImpl、用 resolve_max_message_size 解析 SGLANG_TONIC_PAYLOAD 与默认 64MiB 上限、配置 max_decoding/encoding_message_size、serve_with_incoming_shutdown 接收 Notify 信号，并与 GrpcServerHandle.shutdown 协作。
learning_goals: 理解服务引导流程与消息大小上限的意义(高于 tonic 4MiB 默认)
理解 SGLANG_TONIC_PAYLOAD 的合法/非法/0 值处理与回退
理解优雅关停的 shutdown 信号链(Notify→serve_with_incoming_shutdown)
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 resolve_max_message_size，说明当 SGLANG_TONIC_PAYLOAD 设为非数字字符串、0、合法正数时的不同行为;再从 GrpcServerHandle::shutdown 的 notify_one() 出发，追踪信号如何传递到 serve_with_incoming_shutdown 的异步闭包并最终停止 tonic Server。

lectures.title: Tokio 运行时与 GIL 协作模型
lectures.topic: 讲解并发模型：start_server 在独立 std::thread 里构建多线程 Tokio 运行时并把 handle 交给 PyBridge；Python GIL 调用通过 Python::with_gil / tokio::task::spawn_blocking 完成；回调持有 tokio_handle 以便在通道满时 spawn 异步发送。
learning_goals: 理解 Tokio 运行时与承载它的 OS 线程的关系及为何独占一个线程
理解 GIL 获取时机与为何用 spawn_blocking 包装同步 GIL 调用
理解 PyBridge / 回调为何需要保存并共享 tokio_handle
minimal_modules: tokio_handle 的 clone 与共享
server.rs 中的 spawn_blocking 调用点
lectures.practice_task: 在 server.rs 中找出所有 tokio::task::spawn_blocking 调用(如 health_check/get_model_info/tokenize 的 Python 回退),说明它们为何不能直接在 async fn 里用 Python::with_gil 同步阻塞;再解释 PyBridge 构造时保存 tokio_handle 的用途(提示：与 try_send_chunk 的 Full 分支有关)。

lectures.title: 测试组织与扩展实践：新增 RPC、新增分词器后端
lectures.topic: 讲解单元测试组织(server/tests.rs、bridge/tests.rs、py_utils/tests.rs、request_utils 内联测试)与串行 env 测试模式；并以两个扩展场景收尾——如何新增一个 RPC、如何新增一个 TokenizerBackend。
learning_goals: 掌握现有测试写法与 SGLANG_TONIC_PAYLOAD 这类 env 串行测试的 SAFETY 约定
理解新增一个 gRPC RPC 需要改动的完整清单(proto、server trait 实现、请求字典、错误映射)
理解 tokenizers.rs 的 load_backend 后端扩展点
lectures.practice_task: 参照 request_utils.rs 内联测试(generate_dicts_include_session_id 等)的风格，为 build_text_embed_dict 编写一个单元测试，断言 routing_key 与 trace_headers 被正确写入字典、且空 trace_headers 时不出现 external_trace_header 键;或参照 HuggingFaceTokenizerBackend 为 TokenizerBackend 新增一个占位后端结构体并在 load_backend 中注册探测。
